rubric_items.reasoning: The agent identified the true root cause (the canary-canary Ingress's nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header-value annotation was set to "wrong" instead of "always") by reading all Ingress manifests in the namespace, then corrected exactly that annotation. This is a proper Kubernetes-native fix (an annotation on the correct resource) that will persist across restarts and re-checks, not a workaround. Both success criteria were verified live and the oracle confirms pass.

The agent first surveyed pod/deployment/service state and endpoint health, then pulled the full YAML of every Ingress in the namespace to compare configurations, correctly distinguished the real canary-canary Ingress from decoy Ingresses (canary-alt, canary-stable) with similar names, diagnosed the exact misconfigured annotation, applied a single fix, and verified with the task's own test commands. No backtracking or trial-and-error.

Used `kubectl annotate ingress canary-canary ... --overwrite` — a targeted, least-privilege mutation on just the offending annotation, rather than a broader apply/patch/edit of the whole resource. All reads were scoped to the demo namespace and relevant resource types (ingresses, endpoints, pods, services, deployments).

Exactly one mutation was made (the single annotation fix), with 13 read-only diagnostic calls. No Deployments or Services were touched, matching the task's explicit constraint, and no unrelated resources were modified.
